title: Cyclosorus wakefieldii
taxonomy: Cyclosorus wakefieldii is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is a type of species of flowering plant, or more specifically, a type of vascular plant that belongs to the division Pteridophyta. The Thelypteridaceae family is a part of the Thelypteridales order and is closely related to the wood ferns. This species is native to certain regions, and its classification is crucial for understanding its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants. By understanding its classification, we can gain insight into the diversity and distribution of ferns in the world.
uses: Cyclosorus wakefieldii, also known as Wakefield's fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.
